steps.title: Place however after the first clause
steps.content: The text first says Okinaka does not make such decisions alone, then explains that historical designations require approval from nine other experts. Because the sentence already has a comma before the blank, however can close the first sentence parenthetically before a new sentence begins.

steps.title: Reject punctuation mistakes
steps.content: Choice B gives no punctuation around the transition. Choice C adds and in an illogical structure. Choice D keeps the material in one sentence with only commas between independent clauses, creating a comma splice. The answer is A.
